# scuv

> Centralized Python virtual environment manager â€” pyenv-style workflow powered by uv

<!-- MAINTENANCE NOTE: When updating version or MSRV in Cargo.toml, also update references in this file.
     CI will verify MSRV consistency between Cargo.toml and llms-full.txt. -->

scuv manages Python virtual environments from a single central location (`~/.scuv/`),
combining pyenv-virtualenv's familiar workflow with uv's speed. Written in Rust.

## Installation

Prerequisites: [uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv) and Rust 1.88+

```bash
cargo install scoop-uv
```

### Shell Setup

Add to your shell config and restart:

```bash
# Bash
echo 'eval "$(scuv init bash)"' >> ~/.bashrc

# Zsh
echo 'eval "$(scuv init zsh)"' >> ~/.zshrc

# Fish
echo 'scuv init fish | source' >> ~/.config/fish/config.fish

# PowerShell
Add-Content $PROFILE 'Invoke-Expression (& scuv init powershell)'
```

## Quick Start

```bash
scuv install 3.12           # Install Python 3.12 via uv
scuv create myproject 3.12  # Create a virtualenv
scuv use myproject           # Activate (auto-activates on directory entry)
scuv list                    # List all virtualenvs
scuv remove myproject        # Delete a virtualenv
```

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `scuv list` | List virtualenvs (aliases: `ls`) |
| `scuv list --pythons` | List installed Python versions |
| `scuv list --python-version <VER>` | Filter environments by Python version |
| `scuv list --sort <name|created|last-used>` | Sort order; None-timestamps sort last with name tie-break (Unreleased) |
| `scuv create <name> [version]` | Create virtualenv (default: latest Python) |
| `scuv create <name> --python-path <PATH>` | Create virtualenv with specific Python executable |
| `scuv create <name> <ver> --install-python` | Create env; install Python on demand if missing (v0.11.0) |
| `scuv use <name>` | Set + activate environment |
| `scuv use <name> --global` | Set as global default |
| `scuv use <name> --link` | Also create `.venv` symlink for IDE |
| `scuv use system` | Deactivate, use system Python |
| `scuv use --unset` | Remove version file |
| `scuv remove <name>` | Delete virtualenv (aliases: `rm`, `delete`) |
| `scuv clone <src> <dst>` | Duplicate an env in-place; `--no-packages` for skeleton (v0.11.0) |
| `scuv install [version]` | Install Python version |
| `scuv uninstall <version>` | Remove Python version |
| `scuv uninstall <version> --cascade` | Remove Python version + all environments using it |
| `scuv info <name>` | Show virtualenv details (includes `Last used:` row since Unreleased) |
| `scuv status` | Summarise current state (Active/Configured/System/None) (v0.11.0); includes `Last used:` since Unreleased |
| `scuv which <exe>` | Resolve an executable path inside the active env (v0.11.0) |
| `scuv run <env> -- <cmd>` | Run a command inside an env without activating (v0.11.0) |
| `scuv sync` | Reconcile the active env with `.scuv.toml` manifest (v0.11.0) |
| `scuv export <name>` | Snapshot env as JSON (schema v1) (v0.11.0) |
| `scuv import <file>` | Restore env from JSON snapshot; `--name <new>` to rename (v0.11.0) |
| `scuv doctor` | Health check |
| `scuv doctor --fix` | Auto-fix issues |
| `scuv shell <name>` | Set shell-specific env (temporary) |
| `scuv shell --unset` | Clear shell-specific setting |
| `scuv init <shell>` | Output shell init script |
| `scuv completions <shell>` | Generate completion script |
| `scuv lang [code]` | Get/set language (en, ko, ja, pt-BR) |
| `scuv migrate list` | List migratable envs (pyenv, conda, virtualenvwrapper) |
| `scuv migrate @env <name>` | Migrate single environment |
| `scuv migrate all` | Migrate all environments (parallel via rayon since v0.11.0) |
| `scuv gc` | Garbage-collect orphan virtualenvs; default is dry-run, pass `-y`/`--yes` to remove; `--aggressive` also flags unused uv-managed Pythons; `--older-than <30d|2w|1y>` adds stale envs whose `last_used` is past the cutoff (Unreleased) |
| `scuv prune` | Prune the uv cache (thin wrapper around `uv cache prune`) |
| `scuv verify [NAME]` | Per-env health diagnosis (6 checks). Default always exits 0; `--strict` exits 1 on any issue |
| `scuv man [DIR]` | Generate man pages â€” stdout for top-level, or write `scuv.1` + `scuv-<sub>.1` per subcommand into DIR |

Most commands support `--json` for machine-readable output.

Global options: `--quiet`, `--no-color`

## Key Concepts

### Central Storage

All virtualenvs are stored in `~/.scuv/virtualenvs/`. Override with `SCUV_HOME` env var.

### Set a Specific Python Version as Global Default

To set Python `3.11.0` as the global default for new shells:

```bash
scuv install 3.11.0
scuv create py311 3.11.0
scuv use py311 --global
```

Important: `--global` stores an environment name (`py311`) in `~/.scuv/version`,
not the raw Python version string. Local `.scuv-version` and `SCUV_VERSION`
override the global default.

### Create a Project Environment with Python 3.9.5

```bash
scuv install 3.9.5
scuv create myproject 3.9.5
scuv info myproject
```

If `3.9.5` is not found, check discovery with `uv python list` and
`scuv list --pythons`, then install and retry.

### Uninstall a Python Version and Associated Environments

```bash
scuv list --python-version 3.12
scuv uninstall 3.12 --cascade
scuv list --pythons
scuv doctor
```

Use `--force` for non-interactive workflows.
Without `--cascade`, dependent environments are not removed and may break.

### Project-Scoped Auto-Activation Control

```bash
export SCUV_NO_AUTO=1
unset SCUV_NO_AUTO

scuv use system
scuv use myproject

scuv shell system
scuv shell --unset
```

Avoid `--global` when you want temporary or directory-only behavior.

### Install Dependencies from requirements.txt in Active Environment

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

If needed, use `pip install -r path/to/requirements.txt`.
Verify with `pip list`.

### List Python Versions and Associated Environments

```bash
scuv list --pythons
scuv list
scuv list --python-version 3.12
```

Use `--json` for machine-readable output and `--bare` for shell scripts.

### Integrate Custom or Pre-Existing Python

```bash
scuv create myenv --python-path /opt/python-debug/bin/python3

export PATH="/opt/python-debug/bin:$PATH"
scuv create myenv 3.13
```

Verify with `uv python list`, `scuv info myenv`, and `scuv doctor -v`.
Custom interpreter path is recorded in metadata (`python_path`).

### Version Files

Priority (first match wins):
1. `SCUV_VERSION` env var (shell session override, set by `scuv shell`)
2. `.scuv-version` in current directory (local, walks parent directories)
3. `~/.scuv/version` (global default)

### Shell Integration

scuv outputs shell code to stdout; the shell wrapper `eval`s it (pyenv pattern).
Auto-activation triggers on directory change when `.scuv-version` is present.

Disable auto-activation: `export SCUV_NO_AUTO=1`

### Environment Name Rules

- Pattern: `^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$` (max 64 chars)
- Must start with a letter
- Reserved words: activate, base, completions, create, deactivate, default, delete, global, help, init, install, list, local, remove, resolve, root, system, uninstall, use, version, versions

### Supported Shells

bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell (Core 7.x+ and Windows PowerShell 5.1+)

### Migration Sources

Import environments from pyenv-virtualenv, virtualenvwrapper, and conda.

### Internationalization

Supported languages: English (`en`), Korean (`ko`), Japanese (`ja`), Portuguese-BR (`pt-BR`)

Priority: `SCUV_LANG` env > `~/.scuv/config.json` > system locale > `en`

## Configuration

- Config file: `~/.scuv/config.json`
- Home directory: `~/.scuv/` (override: `SCUV_HOME`)
- Metadata: `~/.scuv/virtualenvs/<name>/.scoop-metadata.json`

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/ai-screams/scoop-uv
- Documentation: https://ai-scream.ai/scoop-uv/
- Crates.io: https://crates.io/crates/scoop-uv
- API Reference: https://docs.rs/scoop-uv
- Full LLM Reference: See llms-full.txt in this repository
